title: Decouple immutable archive history from current complexity thresholds
outOfScope: Deleting or rewriting archived Contract/Summary evidence
Changing PR ownership rules for newly changed archive pairs
Changing the semantics of tracked-file, Python-line, or Markdown-line thresholds
Introducing external cold-storage or release-artifact migration

sources.reason: Current hard thresholds include archiveContracts and archiveSummaries.
The checker currently treats cumulative archive totals as blocking limits.
Defines immutable archive evidence and the complexity-check lifecycle.
Current PR audit is the boundary for changed archive-pair ownership.
Existing regression coverage for policy loading and archive metrics.

riskAssessment.reason: The change removes a cumulative archive-count blocker while preserving archive integrity and PR ownership gates.

executionDecision.reason: The design and scope are confirmed; implementation is limited to the complexity checker, policy, tests, and documentation.

preReviewWarnings: Review that archive integrity and current-PR ownership remain blocking after cumulative archive totals become observational.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The governance complexity checker reports archive Contract/Summary totals but does not compare them with blocking max limits.
The checker continues to fail on missing archive pairs, malformed index entries, and dangling index paths.

Existing non-archive complexity limits remain blocking with only the minimum calibration needed to include this Work Item's required governance documents, and current-PR archive ownership behavior remains unchanged.
Documentation explains that immutable archive history is observational and that current PR evidence is validated separately.
guidelines: Do not weaken current-PR archive ownership validation.

intent.problem: The cumulative immutable archive count has exceeded the fixed complexity threshold, causing current Work Items to fail even though the historical evidence is valid.
constraints: Do not delete or rewrite historical archive evidence.
Do not weaken pairing, index-integrity, or current-PR ownership checks.
Keep the change language-neutral and limited to repository governance behavior.
intent.rationale: Separate immutable historical evidence from live complexity limits so past Work Items remain auditable without blocking unrelated current work.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A repository with archive totals above the old 260 limit passes complexity-limit evaluation when archive integrity is valid.
Missing archive pairs and dangling index references still fail the complexity check.
Current PR archive ownership remains governed by the existing PR audit.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly approved separating immutable archive history from current complexity thresholds.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
